perturbation.original_text: The Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation e-mail distri bution list(s);
perturbation.changed_text: The CPC and TLCF contact list(s);
perturbation.explanation: Changing “The Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation e-mail distribution list(s)” to “The CPC and TLCF contact list(s)” creates ambiguity. While CPC and TLCF are abbreviations for the organizations, it is not explicitly stated in close proximity, introducing an in-text contradiction. It is unclear if 'e-mail distribution list' and 'contact list' are the same thing, especially if the contact list includes phone numbers or other contact information. It brings into question the scope of the confidentiality agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For purposes of this Agreement, “Confidential Information” mean s all information and materials, in whatever form, whether tangible or intangible, disclosed by Companion Paws or The LifeLine Canada Foundation or any of its authorized representatives to Volunteer or Evaluator , or to which Volunteer , Trainer or Evaluator otherwise gains access as a result of volunteering or w orking for Companion Paws or The LifeLine Canada Foundation , pertaining in any manner to the activities of C ompanion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation or its affiliates, consultants, members, or any person or entity to which Companion Paws ad The LifeLine Canada Foundation owes a duty of confidentiality, whether or not labeled or identified as proprietary or confidential. All proprietary information of Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Found ation that is not known generally to the public is Confidential Information. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the following are deemed Confidential Information:  Ideas for research and development;  Information submitted in the investigati on of complai nts or involving ethics cases;  Computer records and software (including software that is proprietary to third parties);  Any other information which Companion Paws Canada and The LifeLine Canada Foundation must keep confidential as a result o f obligations to third parties;  Information regarding the administration of components of the certification programs;  Exam -relate d technologies and components;  Item content, characteristics, development or other aspects of the exam inations and their dev elopment, maintenance and administra tion;  Identities of Certificants, candidates, customers, suppliers, or third party contractors, including without limitation any media, advertising, or public relations firms;  The CPC and TLCF contact list(s);  Human resources data and information about employees, con tractors and other volunteers;  Cost and other financial data;  Pollin g and focus group information;  Any goods or services Volunteer or Training and Home Pr ovider provides to Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation under this Agreement.  Any other information to which Volunteer or Evaluator has access while involved in the Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation activities.  Pictures and V ideos of Companion Paws dogs are only permitted without individuals unless the Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation Waiver has been signed and initialed
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'The Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation e-mail distribution list(s)' to 'The CPC and TLCF contact list(s)'. The explanation states that this change creates ambiguity because it's unclear if 'e-mail distribution list' and 'contact list' are the same thing, potentially including phone numbers or other contact information. The contradicted text explicitly includes 'The CPC and TLCF contact list(s)' as Confidential Information. The change introduces ambiguity about the scope of 'contact list' and its relation to the original 'e-mail distribution list', creating a contradiction regarding what information is considered confidential, thus triggering the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Volunteer, Trainer or Evaluator shall avoid personal and professional conflicts of interest in all matters pertaining to the Companion Paws Certification program. Conflicting interests may include but are not limite d to such areas as financial, personal relationships, and/or professional relationships.
perturbation.changed_text: Service Provider shall avoid personal and professional conflicts of interest in all matters pertaining to the Companion Paws Certification program. Conflicting interests may include but are not limite d to such areas as financial, personal relationships, and/or professional relationships.
perturbation.explanation: The agreement does not define the term 'Service Provider'. By replacing 'Volunteer, Trainer or Evaluator' with 'Service Provider', it is unclear if the obligations and restrictions related to conflicts of interest apply to a new or different group. This creates an in-text contradiction: If 'Service Provider' is meant to encompass 'Volunteer, Trainer or Evaluator', then sections referring specifically to the latter might not apply to all 'Service Providers'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Volunteer , Trainer or Evaluator Code of Conduct From the w ork and integrity of our organization and the services they provide, people will determine our reputation and abilit y to make an impact on the well -being of dogs and their handlers . The obligation of evaluators, trainers and volunteers to those we represent and serve will best be achieved thr ough a common Code of Conduct to guide our p rofessional practice. Therefore , compliance with this Code of Conduct is a requirement for all Companion Paws volunteer s, trainers and evaluators .
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly defines who is subject to conflict of interest rules: 'Volunteer, Trainer or Evaluator'. The changed text replaces this with 'Service Provider', a term not defined in the agreement. Because the agreement does not define what 'Service Provider' encompasses and the Code of Conduct specifically applies to 'Volunteer, Trainer or Evaluator', there is now a contradiction. It is unclear whether the Code of Conduct now applies to the 'Service Provider' and those the code of conduct originally applied to. This creates a strong contradiction as enforcement and compliance become uncertain.

perturbation.original_text: Violation of this Agreement will be g rounds for termination of service with Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation . A Committee Chair plus any two members of the Executive Committee may elect to remove a volunteer or training and home provider at any time.
perturbation.changed_text: Violation of this Agreement will be g rounds for termination of service with Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation . A Committee Chair plus any two members of the Review Board may elect to remove a volunteer or training and home provider at any time.
perturbation.explanation: Changing “Executive Committee” to “Review Board” creates ambiguity, especially since the document doesn't define 'Review Board'. It contradicts the presumed understanding of the original text, which is that the Executive Committee has authority over personnel matters. The contradiction lies in the uncertainty of who has the authority to remove a volunteer, given that earlier sections don't mention the 'Review Board'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Violation of this Agreement will be g rounds for termination of service with Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation . A Committee Chair plus any two members of the Review Board may elect to remove a volunteer or training and home provider at any time. The provisions of thi s Agree ment and Volunteer, Trainer and Evaluator obligations hereunder shall survive any expiration, termination, or rescission of this Agreement an d remain even after Volunteer , Trainer or Evaluator relationship with Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation ends. Except as provided herein, Volunteer , Trainer or Evaluator is prohibited from disclosing or using any confidential information in all circumstances, including but not limited to subsequent engagements or employment with third parties.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Executive Committee" to "Review Board" introduces a contradiction. The original text assumed the Executive Committee had authority, which is now uncertain with the introduction of the 'Review Board', which is not defined in the document, creating ambiguity about who can remove a volunteer. The contradicted text shows that the provision of the agreement should be kept as is, however, by changing the term "Executive Committee" to "Review Board" it introduces a contradiction, based on the explanation provided, about who has the right to remove a volunteer. Therefore there is a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be binding upon Volunteer , Trainer or Evaluator and Volunteer , Trainer or Evaluator personal representatives and successors in interest, and shall i nure to th e benefit of Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation , its successo rs and assigns.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be binding upon the Individual and Individual personal representatives and successors in interest, and shall i nure to th e benefit of Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation , its successo rs and assigns.
perturbation.explanation: Changing “Volunteer, Trainer or Evaluator” to “Individual” introduces a significant contradiction. The contract extensively refers to 'Volunteer, Trainer or Evaluator' with specific obligations and responsibilities. Replacing it with 'Individual' broadens the scope but leaves it unclear if all the previously defined responsibilities still apply. This creates an in-text contradiction concerning the applicability of specific sections (like the Code of Conduct) to this broader 'Individual'. The initial definition of 'Volunteer, Trainer or Evaluator' does not mention 'Individual'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Volunteer , Trainer or Evaluator Code of Conduct From the w ork and integrity of our organization and the services they provide, people will determine our reputation and abilit y to make an impact on the well -being of dogs and their handlers . The obligation of evaluators, trainers and volunteers to those we represent and serve will best be achieved thr ough a common Code of Conduct to guide our p rofessional practice. Therefore , compliance with this Code of Conduct is a requirement for all Companion Paws volunteer s, trainers and evaluators .
perturbation.justification: The original text specifically refers to "Volunteer, Trainer or Evaluator," while the changed text replaces this with "Individual." This change contradicts the "Volunteer, Trainer or Evaluator Code of Conduct" section, which explicitly states that compliance with the Code of Conduct is a requirement for all "Companion Paws volunteers, trainers and evaluators." By changing the term to "Individual," it creates ambiguity as to whether the Code of Conduct still applies to all those previously defined by "Volunteer, Trainer or Evaluator," creating an in-text contradiction as the definition of who needs to follow code of conduct is altered. Thus it aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.
